identity.design_philosophy: Fusion over Imitation. Emotional First. Content is King.

rules.headings: Use Outfit with tight tracking (tracking-tight). Weights: 600 (SemiBold) or 700 (Bold). Avoid generic 'Inter' for headings.
rules.body: Use Public Sans. Relaxed line-height (leading-relaxed) for readability.

usage.primary_action: bg-primary hover:bg-primary-900 text-white shadow-lg shadow-primary/20 transition-all active:scale-95

grid_system.hero_split: grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-8 lg:gap-12 items-start

DonationCard.style: Sticky on desktop (top-24). Glassmorphism effect or solid white with deep shadow. High hierarchy.

ProgressBar.animation: Fill width with transition-all duration-1000 ease-out. Add a 'shimmer' overlay animation.

ShareButtons.style: Inline flex gap-3. Icon-only circles or pills with text. Minimalist.

HeroSection.style: Immersive image on left/top. Strong Headline. Impact badge.
HeroSection.image_treatment: Rounded-2xl or 3xl. Subtle parallax if possible.

assets.icons: lucide-react (default). Use specific icons: Heart, Share2, Users, TrendingUp, ShieldCheck, GraduationCap.

interactions.scroll: Use IntersectionObserver to trigger fade-ins for sections.

accessibility.aria: Progress bars must have aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax. Buttons must have aria-label if icon-only.

instructions_to_main_agent: Do NOT override tailwind.config.js unless absolutely necessary. Use arbitrary values if specific spacing needed.
Implement the 'DonationCard' as a separate component that is sticky on desktop.
Use 'framer-motion' for the progress bar filling animation and section entrance animations.
Ensure the 'Share' functionality actually opens the respective platform's share URL (e.g., wa.me, t.me).
For the 'Recent Donors' list, use a marquee or a smooth vertical scroll if there are many, or just a static list of top 5.
Use the provided Unsplash URLs for the campaign images.
Add 'data-testid' to all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links).
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES: Avoid generic centered layouts. Use asymmetry.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
